title: Presigned Downloads, CloudFront Signatures, Cookies, and Revocation · Program Defense
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for presigned downloads, cloudfront signatures, cookies, and revocation and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for time-bounded signed-delivery decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.
Choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
Choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Signed Access Revocation
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 3579 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound signed access by time, resource, key group, and explicit SHA-256 review; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Signed Access Revocation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Signed Access Revocation, keep Signed Access Revocation connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz S3 Presigned Download, challenge the documented misconception with file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: A presigned S3 URL has permissions independent from the signing principal.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Signed Access Revocation
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Signed Access Revocation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 8338 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound signed access by time, resource, key group, and explicit SHA-256 review; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Signed Access Revocation, keep Signed Access Revocation connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model expiry-at-request-start, range continuation, key-group rotation, authorization changes, logs and referrers, cache behavior, and emergency revocation limits.
